title: Opening — Routine Referral to endodontist
brief: Pick the strongest opening sentence for a routine referral of Mr James Patel (58) with newly diagnosed advanced periodontitis to Dr Sara Kim, endodontist.

scenario.purpose: Routine referral for assessment and management of newly diagnosed advanced periodontitis

choices.text: I am writing to refer Mr James Patel, a 58-year-old man with newly diagnosed advanced periodontitis, for your assessment and management.
choices.rationale: States the purpose immediately, identifies patient, names the diagnosis, and tells the endodontist what is requested.

choices.text: Mr James Patel is a 58-year-old gentleman who has been registered at this practice for many years and recently developed an irregular heartbeat.
choices.rationale: Patient-first opening; the purpose is delayed and the practice-history detail is irrelevant.

choices.text: Hi Sara, hope you are well — could you take a quick look at this patient when you get a moment?
choices.rationale: Informal salutation, no patient identification, no diagnosis, no clear request.

choices.text: Pt 58M new AF. Plz assess + start anticoag.
choices.rationale: Note form is forbidden in OET letters; abbreviations and missing articles fail Genre/Style and Language.

stubNote: This profession variant was templated from rulebooks/writing/drills/medicine/. Rule scaffolding is correct; specific clinical scenarios should be reviewed by Dr. Ahmed Hesham in a future authoring pass.
